title: Personal Website
tagline: This website — Go + HTMX with live LLM demos
description: A portfolio website built with Go and HTMX featuring interactive AI demos powered by a self-hosted RTX 4090. Includes an LLM playground with SSE streaming, model benchmarking, RAG demonstrations, and vision analysis. Designed for sub-millisecond server renders with no JavaScript frameworks.
features: Token-by-token SSE streaming from Ollama
GPU/CPU failover with real-time status monitoring
Interactive RAG and Vision demos
Company-specific access gating with analytics
Sub-millisecond server-side rendering
Dark theme, mobile-first responsive design

title: 86 Challenge Telemetry
tagline: Telemetry analysis for GR86/BRZ time trial community
description: A web application for the 86 Challenge time trial community. Parses VBO telemetry files from Vbox GPS loggers, provides lap analysis with split times, driver comparisons, and a grip simulation engine that models optimal laps based on recorded telemetry data.
features: VBO telemetry file parsing and analysis
Interactive track maps with Leaflet.js
Lap comparison with split-time breakdowns
Grip simulation engine for optimal lap modeling
Driver profiles and event management
Automated event scraping from 86challenge.us

title: Ollama Inference Proxy
tagline: GPU/CPU failover proxy for Ollama with model rewriting
description: A lightweight Go reverse proxy that sits in front of multiple Ollama instances. Routes requests to a GPU backend (RTX 4090 via WSL2) with automatic failover to a CPU backend. Rewrites model names on failover to use smaller, CPU-appropriate models.
features: Health-check based GPU/CPU failover
Automatic model rewriting on CPU fallback
Transparent streaming proxy (SSE/NDJSON)
Sub-3s health check timeout
Single binary, zero dependencies

title: Homelab Infrastructure
tagline: Multi-node Proxmox cluster with 30+ containers and GPU inference
description: A self-hosted infrastructure running on three physical machines: an HP Elite Mini (Proxmox node), an HP Workstation (Proxmox node), and a gaming PC with an RTX 4090 for GPU inference. The two Proxmox nodes form a cluster running 6+ VMs and multiple LXC containers across VLANs, hosting 30+ Docker containers for media, productivity, home automation, and AI workloads. External access is managed through Traefik reverse proxy, Cloudflare Tunnels, and a Nebula mesh VPN spanning homelab, OCI cloud, and a travel router.
diagram:
┌──────────────────┐
                    │ Cloudflare       │
                    │ Tunnel + DNS     │
                    └────────┬─────────┘
                             │
  ┌──────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────┐
  │                   Home Network                      │
  │                                                     │
  │  HP Elite Mini (Proxmox)   HP Workstation (Proxmox) │
  │  ├─ VM: homelab (31ct)     ├─ VM: radio             │
  │  ├─ VM: home-assistant     ├─ CT: adguard           │
  │  ├─ VM: traefik proxy      └─ CT: ollama-proxy ─┐   │
  │  ├─ CT: adguard (x2)                            │   │
  │  ├─ CT: mqtt                                    │   │
  │  └─ CT: zigbee2mqtt                             │   │
  │                                                 │   │
  │  Gaming PC (RTX 4090, Windows + WSL2)           │   │
  │  └─ Ollama (GPU inference) ◄────────────────────┘   │
  │     gemma3:12b, qwen3:8b, llama3.1:8b, ...          │
  │                                                     │
  │  Traefik ◄── *.ryzhkov.dev (:80/:443)               │
  └──────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────┘
                         │ Nebula VPN
             ┌───────────┴───────────┐
             │                       │
       ┌─────┴─────┐          ┌──────┴──────┐
       │ OCI Cloud │          │ GL.iNet     │
       │ Lighthouse│          │ Travel      │
       │ Caddy     │          │ Router      │
       └───────────┘          └─────────────┘
features: 2-node Proxmox VE cluster (HP Elite Mini + HP Workstation) with shared storage
Gaming PC (RTX 4090) running Ollama for GPU-accelerated LLM inference via WSL2
6+ VMs: main homelab server (31 Docker containers), Home Assistant OS, Traefik proxy, and more
LXC containers: AdGuard DNS (x2), Mosquitto MQTT, Zigbee2MQTT, Ollama proxy
VLAN-segmented networking: management (VLAN 1), public-facing (VLAN 20), services (VLAN 70), gaming PC (VLAN 90)
30+ Docker services: Immich, Authentik, Forgejo, Miniflux, Hugo blog, n8n, Uptime Kuma, and more
Nebula mesh VPN connecting homelab, OCI lighthouse, and travel router
Traefik reverse proxy with Let's Encrypt for *.ryzhkov.dev, Caddy on OCI
Cloudflare Tunnels for secure external access without WAN exposure
homelab-ctl: custom Go TUI/MCP tool for managing Docker containers across all VMs with vim-style navigation
Automated backups to Backblaze B2 via rclone (2x weekly)
Home Assistant with Zigbee (ZBT-2), MQTT, and smart home automations
